parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Co-amoxiclav — perioperative anaphylaxis cause'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'Sudden hypotension with increased airway pressure after drug administration is highly suggestive of perioperative anaphylaxis with bronchospasm.'}

{"topic": "Primary Frca Sample Sbas", "exam": "primary", "curriculum_node_code": null, "model": "openai:gpt-5.5", "generated_at": "2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172", "questions": [{"stem": "An otherwise healthy 21-year-old woman is anaesthetised for laparoscopic appendicectomy. After rapid sequence induction with fentanyl, propofol and rocuronium, she receives co-amoxiclav, gentamicin, morphine and ondansetron. After skin preparation and before incision, her blood pressure falls and the ventilator high-pressure alarm sounds.", "lead": "Which agent is the most likely cause of this clinical deterioration?", "options": [{"text": "Chlorhexidine", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Chlorhexidine is an important cause of perioperative anaphylaxis, but in this timing and context it is less common than antibiotics."}, {"text": "Co-amoxiclav", "is_correct": true, "explanation": "The combination of hypotension and bronchospasm suggests anaphylaxis; antibiotics are the commonest perioperative cause, and co-amoxiclav is a frequent culprit."}, {"text": "Gentamicin", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Gentamicin can cause allergic reactions but is less commonly implicated than beta-lactam antibiotics such as co-amoxiclav."}, {"text": "Morphine", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Morphine can cause non-IgE histamine release with hypotension or bronchospasm, but severe perioperative anaphylaxis is more likely due to antibiotics."}, {"text": "Rocuronium", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Neuromuscular blocking agents are a common cause of perioperative anaphylaxis, but antibiotics are slightly more frequent overall and co-amoxiclav is the most likely listed agent."}], "explanation": "Sudden hypotension with increased airway pressure after drug administration is highly suggestive of perioperative anaphylaxis with bronchospasm. NAP6 identified antibiotics as the most frequent cause of perioperative anaphylaxis, with co-amoxiclav commonly implicated.", "curriculum_node_codes": ["1_GA_E_18", "1_GA_A_8", "1_GA_B_3", "1_GA_B_17", "1_GA_B_4"], "curriculum_node_scores": [0.7931595056105607, 0.7808902710360073, 0.7364008005026488, 0.7196253273375248, 0.7138077408071251], "title": "Co-amoxiclav — perioperative anaphylaxis cause"}]}
